summary: Validated NPI Luhn validation (Task Prompt 8) across backend + frontend. All 30 tests in test_npi_validation.py pass (29 passed, 1 skipped by design per the known-invalid parametrisation that delegates to test_trims_whitespace). Regression suite (test_professional_licenses.py, test_profile_self_service.py, test_password_change_hardening.py, test_pin_security.py, test_reauth_pin_step_up.py, test_security_hardening.py) = 94 passed. Combined = 123 passed, 1 skipped (matches expected). Frontend NpiCard on /security?tab=licenses correctly enforces 10-digit Luhn validation with distinct error messages, disables Save until valid, strips non-digits on input, exposes aria-invalid/aria-describedby, renders the NPPES disclaimer verbatim, saves successfully, and value persists across reload.

issues: HTML maxLength=10 on npi-input clips a pasted string with dashes (e.g. '12-34-56-7893' = 13 chars) to the first 10 chars BEFORE the onChange strip runs, so the user ends up with 7 digits (1234567) instead of all 10 (1234567893). Minor UX polish — user just retypes the last 3 digits — but strictly the spec says 'pasting 12-34-56-7893 with dashes strips to digits'. Consider removing the HTML maxLength attribute and relying on the JS `.replace(/\D/g,'').slice(0,10)` that already runs in onChange.

action_items: Optional UX polish: on /app/frontend/src/pages/account/LicensesTab.jsx NpiCard, drop `maxLength={10}` from the Input (keep the JS slice) so a pasted '12-34-56-7893' becomes the full '1234567893' instead of being truncated to '1234567'. Non-blocking; all functional checks otherwise pass.
critical_code_review_comments: core/npi.py is clean, well-documented, pure-function, raises specific error messages per failure mode — good separation of concerns.
Frontend utils/npi.js mirrors the backend exactly; both computeCheckDigit implementations agree on published examples (123456789→3, 000000000→6).
NpiCard correctly treats empty string as 'clear the field' (valid + triggers server-side set-to-null path). Confirmed by test_empty_string_clears_value + frontend reload.

data-testids preserved (npi-card, npi-input, npi-save-btn) + new ones added (npi-error, npi-disclaimer). Accessibility: aria-invalid flips correctly, aria-describedby points to #npi-help. Disclaimer copy is verbatim — does NOT claim NPPES registration.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests create ephemeral `npi_<uuid>@ccms.app` doctors via admin user-create endpoint (not cleaned up — matches existing test pattern in test_professional_licenses.py).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Task Prompt 8 is functionally complete. If retesting the paste behaviour, note that page.fill() in Playwright can look like a real paste but the HTML maxLength attribute still truncates; drop maxLength={10} from npi-input if you want the full 10-digit strip-on-paste experience.
rca of the issue: Paste truncation: Input has both (a) JS onChange strips non-digits then slice(0,10), and (b) HTML maxLength=10. The HTML attribute wins during paste because the browser truncates the pasted string to 10 chars BEFORE firing onChange, so '12-34-56-7893' → '12-34-56-7' → stripped to '1234567'. Fix: drop maxLength from the JSX; JS already caps length.
